notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. RCA at deliver/rca.md is the authoritative specification — user-reviewed and APPROVED 2026-04-28 in Phase 2 before roadmap creation. Approved fix shape: introduce `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_secs(1)` in `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` and materialise the deadline (`tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`) into `next_view.next_attempt_at` in the `RestartAllocation` emission branch. Promotion to exponential backoff is explicitly OUT of scope per RCA §Spec source — it's a separate spec amendment if/when wanted.

Two-step Outside-In TDD. Step 01-01 writes three failing regression tests (RED — committed with --no-verify per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds). Step 01-02 lands the cohesive fix: define `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`, insert the `next_attempt_at` write in the emission branch, and update the `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` docstring to cite the named constant — all in one commit.

RED via compile failure (preferred shape). Step 01-01's three new tests reference `overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`, which does NOT yet resolve against current main. The compile failure IS the RED state — it pins the exact symbol the GREEN step must publish AND prevents any shadow constant from surviving. The crafter must NOT introduce a parallel test-only constant to satisfy the import; the import is held to fail until 01-02 lands the production constant. This is documented up front so the DES PREPARE/RED phases handle the compile-error state cleanly.

Test placement: all three new `#[test]` functions append to the existing acceptance file `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs`. That file already has the imports + fixtures we need (`JobLifecycle`, `JobLifecycleView`, `JobSpec`, `AllocStatusRow`, `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING`, the `tick(...)` / `mk_failed_terminated(...)` helpers, `BTreeMap` etc.) and is registered through `tests/acceptance.rs`'s inline `mod acceptance { ... }` block. No new file, no new module declaration, no new feature flag — the additions are purely test-function appends to an already-wired entrypoint.

Default lane: the regression tests are pure unit-shaped tests against `JobLifecycle::reconcile` (sync over typed inputs — no I/O, no SimDriver, no real infra). They run in the default nextest lane, NOT behind the `integration-tests` feature. This matches the existing tests in the same file (`restart_emitted_when_now_equals_deadline` etc.) which are also default-lane.

Reconciler purity per .claude/rules/development.md §Reconciler I/O: the fix uses `tick.now` exclusively, never `Instant::now()` / `SystemTime::now()`. dst-lint will catch violations at PR time — verify with `cargo xtask dst-lint`. The deadline write is a pure derived value (`tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`) on `NextView`; no async, no I/O, no banned APIs.

The three new test functions partition the missing coverage cleanly: (a) `fresh_failure_writes_deadline_into_next_view` — load-bearing single-tick assertion that `next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` AND `next_view.restart_counts[alloc_id] == 1`; this is the assertion that kills the 'wrote nothing' mutation. (b) `subsequent_tick_within_backoff_window_emits_nothing` — chain-link test demonstrating the user-observable bug: tick-1 reconcile produces `next_view`; tick-2 reconcile (with `tick.now` advanced by less than `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`) is passed that `next_view` as its new `view`, and must emit empty actions with unchanged `restart_counts`. Applied to current main this test FAILS because the deadline is never written → tick-2 re-emits restart. (c) `tick_after_backoff_elapsed_emits_restart_and_advances_deadline` — same chain-link shape; tick-2 advances by more than `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`; restart MUST emit AND `next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == new_tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` (the deadline rolls forward each retry).

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the constant + the `next_attempt_at.insert(...)` call + the `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` docstring fix all land in 01-02's single commit. No deprecation, no shadow constant, no two-phase rollout. The commit contains exactly the three production edits the RCA prescribes, plus the regression tests' transition from RED to GREEN by virtue of the production landing.

Risk to flag for the crafter: any pre-existing DST scenario or acceptance test with a `SimDriver::always_fail` workload that advances `tick.now` by sub-second increments may now hit the new gate before reaching the count ceiling. Per RCA §Risk: those scenarios were exercising the bug, not the spec — adjust the scenario's tick advancement to step past `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` between attempts, do not weaken the assertion. The acceptance test `repeatedly_crashing_workload_exhausts_backoff_and_stops_retrying` is named in the RCA as the most likely candidate for cadence adjustment. Run `cargo xtask dst` and the full nextest workspace as part of GREEN to surface any such case immediately.

Mutation testing scope (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per testing.md §Mutation testing exception): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs`. Kill-rate gate ≥80%. The Arm-A assertion in `fresh_failure_writes_deadline_into_next_view` (`next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`) is the killer for the 'wrote `Duration::ZERO`' mutation. The chain-link tests' second-tick assertions (empty actions in (b), restart in (c)) jointly kill the 'skipped the gate' / 'flipped the comparator' mutation classes. If any mutation survives, add an explicit case to the test file that disambiguates it; do not weaken the assertion to satisfy the gate. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome.

Spec-document edits explicitly OUT of scope per RCA §Files affected and the user-facing prompt's 'What NOT to do' guardrail. `docs/feature/phase-1-first-workload/discuss/user-stories.md` and `docs/feature/phase-1-first-workload/distill/test-scenarios.md` already prescribe the correct behaviour at the spec layer; the implementation just didn't realise the write side. Spec amendments to add an explicit timing-gate scenario at the spec layer are a separate PR if the team wants belt-and-braces.

reviews.scope: RCA + proposed fix direction (constant `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` matching user-stories.md:424 SSOT; materialise deadline in `RestartAllocation` emission branch; regression test arms A + B + C pinning the read-side gate AND the write-side deadline materialisation). Scope: bug fix + regression tests only.
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause (write-side never implemented; field was inert from first commit); approved approach (single constant `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION = Duration::from_secs(1)`, NOT exponential — exponential is a separate spec-amendment PR if/when wanted); approved scope (bug fix + three new regression tests in the existing acceptance file, no spec edits, no new feature flags).

phases.name: Make RestartAllocation branch materialise next_attempt_at deadline
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 appends three new `#[test]` functions to `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` that pin the missing write-side: (a) `fresh_failure_writes_deadline_into_next_view` — load-bearing assertion that `next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` AND `next_view.restart_counts[alloc_id] == 1` after a single tick on a Terminated-failed alloc with empty `view.next_attempt_at`; (b) `subsequent_tick_within_backoff_window_emits_nothing` — chain-link test (the user-observable bug shape) where tick-2's reconcile receives tick-1's `next_view` as its `view` and asserts empty actions + unchanged restart_counts when `tick.now` advances by less than `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`; (c) `tick_after_backoff_elapsed_emits_restart_and_advances_deadline` — same chain-link shape with `tick.now` advancing past the deadline; assert restart emits AND deadline rolls forward to `new_tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`. The new tests reference `overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`, which does NOT yet resolve — the test file fails to compile against current main, and the compile failure IS the RED state (committed with --no-verify per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds). Step 01-02 lands the cohesive fix in one commit: (1) `pub const RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_secs(1)` adjacent to `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING` in `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs`; (2) `next_view.next_attempt_at.insert(failed.alloc_id.clone(), tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION)` between the `restart_counts` increment and the `return` in the `RestartAllocation` emission branch (currently lines 1151-1156); (3) `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` docstring (lines 1262-1263) updated to replace prose `backoff_duration` with the named constant `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`. Step 01-01's three tests flip from compile-failed → GREEN within this commit. Existing tests in the same file (`restart_suppressed_when_now_strictly_before_deadline`, `restart_emitted_when_now_equals_deadline`, `restart_emitted_when_now_strictly_after_deadline`) continue to pass because they pre-seed `next_attempt_at` and the new write does not affect their setup. `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core`, `cargo xtask dst`, and `cargo xtask dst-lint` all green.

steps.name: RED regression tests — RestartAllocation branch must materialise next_attempt_at deadline
steps.description: Append three new `#[test]` functions to the existing acceptance file `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs`. The file already has every fixture and import needed (the existing tests `restart_suppressed_when_now_strictly_before_deadline`, `restart_emitted_when_now_equals_deadline`, `restart_emitted_when_now_strictly_after_deadline` are next-door siblings exercising the read-side gate with hand-seeded `next_attempt_at`). The MISSING coverage is the write-side: that `reconcile`'s `RestartAllocation` emission branch *populates* `next_view.next_attempt_at`. Three additions: (a) `fresh_failure_writes_deadline_into_next_view` — GIVEN a Terminated failed alloc with `view.next_attempt_at` empty AND `view.restart_counts` empty (i.e. attempts=0 for this alloc_id), WHEN `reconcile` runs once at `tick.now = T0`, THEN `actions == [Action::RestartAllocation { alloc_id: <failed>, .. }]` AND `next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == T0 + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` AND `next_view.restart_counts[alloc_id] == 1`. This is the load-bearing single-tick assertion — kills the 'wrote nothing' / 'wrote Duration::ZERO' mutations. (b) `subsequent_tick_within_backoff_window_emits_nothing` — chain-link / two-tick test that demonstrates the user-observable bug. Tick-1: same setup as (a); call reconcile once and capture `(actions_1, next_view_1)`. Tick-2: pass `next_view_1` as the new `view`, with the SAME `actual` (alloc still Terminated-failed) and `tick.now = T0 + Duration::from_millis(500)` (i.e. less than `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`). Assert `actions_2.is_empty()` AND `next_view_2.restart_counts[alloc_id] == 1` (unchanged from tick-1) AND `next_view_2.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == T0 + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` (deadline preserved, not overwritten by no-op tick). Applied to current main this test FAILS because tick-1's `next_view_1.next_attempt_at` is empty → tick-2's gate sees no deadline → tick-2 re-emits `RestartAllocation`. (c) `tick_after_backoff_elapsed_emits_restart_and_advances_deadline` — same chain-link shape as (b), but tick-2's `tick.now = T0 + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION + Duration::from_millis(1)`. Assert `actions_2 == [Action::RestartAllocation { alloc_id, .. }]` AND `next_view_2.restart_counts[alloc_id] == 2` AND `next_view_2.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == (T0 + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION + Duration::from_millis(1)) + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` (the deadline rolls forward to `new_tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`, not `T0 + 2*RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` — this is the assertion that kills any 'rolled deadline from previous deadline rather than from new tick.now' mutation). All three tests import `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` from `overdrive_core::reconciler` (the production module). That import does NOT yet resolve against current main — the test file fails to compile. The compile failure IS the RED state. The crafter MUST NOT introduce a parallel test-only constant or `const _: Duration = ...` shadow inside the test file to make the import resolve; the import is held to fail until 01-02 lands the production constant. Verify RED with `cargo check -p overdrive-core --tests` (compile error: `unresolved import overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`). The commit MUST be made with `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds; the commit body MUST cite that rule by name AND name Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart. No production-code edits in this step. No new modules, no new feature flags, no new files. Commit trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`.
steps.criteria: Three new `#[test]` functions appended to `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` (`fresh_failure_writes_deadline_into_next_view`, `subsequent_tick_within_backoff_window_emits_nothing`, `tick_after_backoff_elapsed_emits_restart_and_advances_deadline`). Each references `overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` directly (no shadow constant, no test-local rename). RED verification: `cargo check -p overdrive-core --tests` errors with `unresolved import overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` (NOT a typo or unrelated import). No production-code files touched. No new modules, files, or feature flags. Commit message: `test(reconciler): pin RestartAllocation branch must materialise next_attempt_at deadline (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit uses `git commit --no-verify` and the body explicitly cites .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Symptom — the chain-link test (b) `subsequent_tick_within_backoff_window_emits_nothing` is the artefact that demonstrates the user-observable bug: against current main, tick-2 within the backoff window re-emits `RestartAllocation` because `next_view.next_attempt_at` is never populated. Step 01-01's RED state pins this exact regression.

RCA §5 Whys WHY 5 (root cause: write-side never implemented) — test (a) `fresh_failure_writes_deadline_into_next_view` directly asserts the missing write (`next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`); this is the load-bearing single-tick assertion that 01-02's GREEN must satisfy.

RCA §Approved fix Arm B (the deadline-written assertion is the specific gap that turned this bug into dead code) — covered by test (a)'s `next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` assertion AND by test (c)'s deadline-roll-forward assertion.

Tests live in the default nextest lane (no `integration-tests` feature gate) — matching the existing reconcile-branch tests in the same file per .claude/rules/testing.md §What stays in the default lane (pure unit-shaped, no I/O, no SimDriver).

RED verified at compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-core --tests` errors on `unresolved import overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`. The compile failure IS the RED state; the crafter does NOT introduce a parallel test-only constant or shadow.
No production-code edits (production fix lands in 01-02). No new modules, files, or feature flags introduced — the three new `#[test]` functions append to an already-wired acceptance entrypoint.
Commit uses `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds; commit body explicitly cites the rule and names Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
steps.tier: Tier 1 (acceptance — default nextest lane, pure unit-shaped over `JobLifecycle::reconcile` typed inputs, no I/O, no SimDriver, no feature flag).
quality_gates: RED verified at the compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-core --tests` errors with `unresolved import overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`. Any other compile error (typo, missing fixture import) is a step-author bug — fix and re-verify.

All three test function bodies reference `overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` directly; no shadow `const RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` or test-local rename in the test file (verified by `git grep -n 'RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION' crates/overdrive-core/tests/` returning only `use` / direct references, never `const RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION:` definitions).
No production-code paths touched: `git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the test file modified, no `src/` paths.

No new env-var / cwd / process-global mutation introduced — `#[serial_test::serial(env)]` is NOT needed; the new tests use plain typed inputs (`JobLifecycleView`, `JobSpec`, `BTreeMap`, `TickContext`).
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and explicit `--no-verify` rationale citing .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
deliverables: Single commit with `--no-verify` and `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit body cites .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and names Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix; verify the existing reconcile-branch tests in `job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` to mirror their fixture shape (the `tick(...)` helper and `mk_failed_terminated(...)` / equivalent fixture builder are already in scope; the next-door tests `restart_emitted_when_now_equals_deadline` etc. are templates for setup but pre-seed `view.next_attempt_at` — the new tests deliberately leave it empty in arm (a) and rely on tick-1's output for arms (b)+(c)). Confirm `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING` is publicly exported from `overdrive_core::reconciler` (it is — used by the existing tests) so the new constant can follow the same `pub const` shape. RED_ACCEPTANCE — write the three test functions. Each starts with the same fixture shape as the existing branch tests (construct desired `State`, actual `State` with the failed Terminated alloc, the `JobLifecycleView`, and the `TickContext { now, tick, deadline }`). For arm (a), `view.next_attempt_at` is `BTreeMap::new()` and `view.restart_counts` is empty for the alloc id; assert on the returned `next_view` directly. For arms (b)+(c), call `reconcile` once to get `(actions_1, next_view_1)`, then construct a NEW `TickContext` with `tick.now` advanced and call `reconcile` again passing `next_view_1` as the second `view`; assert on `(actions_2, next_view_2)`. Use `Duration::from_millis(500)` for arm (b)'s sub-window advance and `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION + Duration::from_millis(1)` for arm (c)'s post-window advance — both expressed in terms of the production constant so the test stays correct under any future tweak to the constant's value. RED_UNIT — N/A (no internal helpers). GREEN — SKIPPED in this step. COMMIT — `git commit --no-verify` with body: `RED scaffold per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits. The three new acceptance tests reference \`overdrive_core::reconciler::RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION\`, which lands in Step 01-02 along with the \`next_attempt_at\` write in the \`RestartAllocation\` emission branch. The compile failure is the RED state; the crafter must not introduce a shadow constant. Step-ID: 01-01`. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. Use `BTreeMap::new()` literally where empty maps are needed (matches existing fixture style in the file).

steps.name: GREEN cohesive fix — RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION constant + materialise deadline in RestartAllocation branch
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Approved fix in a single cohesive commit. Production changes to `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs`: (1) Add `pub const RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_secs(1);` adjacent to the existing `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING` constant (currently around line 1035). The constant MUST be `pub` so test code can import it directly; visibility matches `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING`'s pattern. RCA §Approved fix item 1 is the rationale: 1-second window covers transient hiccups within Phase 1's single-node envelope; constant 1s × ceiling 5 = ~5s wall-clock to 'Failed (backoff exhausted)'. (2) In the `RestartAllocation` emission branch of `JobLifecycle::reconcile` (currently `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1151-1156`), insert the deadline-materialisation write between the `restart_counts` increment and the `return`: `next_view.next_attempt_at.insert(failed.alloc_id.clone(), tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION);`. The exact placement: after `next_view.restart_counts.insert(failed.alloc_id.clone(), <new_count>);` and before the branch's emit/return, so that BOTH `restart_counts` and `next_attempt_at` are updated in the same emission. The write uses `tick.now` (NOT `Instant::now()`) per .claude/rules/development.md §Reconciler I/O — the dst-lint gate enforces this at PR time. (3) Update `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at`'s rustdoc comment (currently lines 1262-1263) to replace the prose `backoff_duration` with the named constant `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` so the docstring and the implementation cite the same identifier. Test verification within this commit: Step 01-01's three new tests transition from RED (compile-failed against current main) to GREEN (compile + pass) without modification — the `unresolved import` error is closed by adding the `pub const`, and the runtime assertions pass because the production write is now in place. Existing tests in `job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` (`restart_suppressed_when_now_strictly_before_deadline`, `restart_emitted_when_now_equals_deadline`, `restart_emitted_when_now_strictly_after_deadline`) continue to pass — they pre-seed `view.next_attempt_at` and assert the read-side gate; the new write does not affect their setup. Pre-existing acceptance and DST tests across the workspace also remain GREEN; per RCA §Risk, the candidate test for cadence adjustment is `repeatedly_crashing_workload_exhausts_backoff_and_stops_retrying` — if that test (or any other with a `SimDriver::always_fail` workload at sub-second tick advancement) now hits the new gate before reaching the count ceiling, adjust the test fixture's tick cadence to step past `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` between attempts; do NOT weaken the assertion. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the constant + the write + the docstring fix all land in this single commit. No deprecation, no shadow constant survives, no two-phase rollout. Commit message: `fix(reconciler): materialise next_attempt_at deadline in RestartAllocation branch` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit): (1) `pub const RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_secs(1);` defined in `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` adjacent to `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING`. (2) `next_view.next_attempt_at.insert(failed.alloc_id.clone(), tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION);` inserted in the `RestartAllocation` emission branch between the `restart_counts` increment and the `return` (currently around lines 1151-1156). (3) `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` rustdoc (currently lines 1262-1263) cites `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` by name (replacing prose `backoff_duration`). Test verification: (4) Step 01-01's three tests flip RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (proving the fix is load-bearing AND the tests pin the invariant). (5) All existing tests in `job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` remain GREEN (the new write is additive — empty `next_attempt_at` setups in those tests are unchanged because their fixtures pre-seed the field). (6) `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (typecheck on macOS) exits 0; full integration run inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI) `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (the deadline write uses `tick.now`, not `Instant::now()`, so the gate stays clean). (7) `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on `overdrive-core`. (8) Mutation gate (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per testing.md §Mutation testing exception): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` reports kill rate ≥80%; no surviving mutation on the new `next_attempt_at.insert` line, no surviving mutation on the `tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` arithmetic, no surviving mutation on the `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` constant value. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Single-cut migration: `git grep 'backoff_duration' crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` returns no matches outside the rustdoc reference to `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`; no `#[deprecated]` aliases. Commit message: `fix(reconciler): materialise next_attempt_at deadline in RestartAllocation branch` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`). Post-GREEN smoke check: `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` shows exactly one production file modified (`crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs`) plus the test file from 01-01's commit (already landed as the prior commit).
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Symptom (closure) — `RestartAllocation` is no longer emitted on every 100ms tick within the 1-second backoff window; the gate (read-side, lines 1145-1149) now sees a populated `next_attempt_at` after the first emission and short-circuits subsequent ticks until the deadline elapses.
RCA §5 Whys WHY 5 (root cause closure) — the write-side companion to the read-side gate is now implemented; `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` is no longer inert.
RCA §Approved fix items 1, 2, 3 — all three production edits land in this commit (constant, write, docstring).
Step 01-01's three tests flip RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (the `unresolved import` error closes; the runtime assertions pass).
All existing tests in `job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` remain GREEN — the read-side tests' pre-seeded `view.next_attempt_at` setups are unchanged.

All pre-existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS; the full integration run passes inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI); `cargo xtask dst` passes. Per RCA §Risk: any pre-existing scenario with sub-second tick advancement that now hits the gate had its tick cadence adjusted (the load-bearing assertion was NOT weakened) — `repeatedly_crashing_workload_exhausts_backoff_and_stops_retrying` is the named candidate.

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix items 1-3 and §Risk; confirm current line numbers in `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs` (the RCA cites lines 1035 for `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING`, 1132-1157 for the `RestartAllocation` emission branch, 1151-1156 for the specific lines to insert into, and 1262-1263 for the `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` rustdoc — line numbers may have drifted slightly; locate by symbol name, not line number); confirm `Duration` is already in scope at the constant's insertion site (the existing `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING` is `usize`, so `Duration` may need `use std::time::Duration;` if not already imported — check the existing imports first). Use Serena's symbolic tools (`find_symbol`, `replace_symbol_body`, etc.) for the production edit — the change is localised to two named symbols (`RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` constant, `JobLifecycle::reconcile` method body) plus a rustdoc comment, all of which are addressable symbolically. RED_ACCEPTANCE — Step 01-01 already wrote the outermost RED tests; verify they currently fail at compile time (`cargo check -p overdrive-core --tests` errors with `unresolved import`). RED_UNIT — N/A (no internal helpers introduced; the deadline write is a single one-line `BTreeMap::insert` call inline in the existing branch — per .claude/rules/development.md \"Don't add features, refactor, or introduce abstractions beyond what the task requires\"). GREEN — implement in this order: (1) Add `pub const RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_secs(1);` adjacent to `RESTART_BACKOFF_CEILING`. Verify `Duration` import is in scope; if not, add `use std::time::Duration;` to the file's imports. (2) Locate the `RestartAllocation` emission branch in `JobLifecycle::reconcile` (search by the existing `next_view.restart_counts.insert(...)` pattern; it sits between `restart_counts` mutation and the branch's `return`/emit). Insert `next_view.next_attempt_at.insert(failed.alloc_id.clone(), tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION);` immediately after the `restart_counts.insert` call. Use `failed.alloc_id.clone()` to match the cloning pattern of the adjacent line. (3) Update `JobLifecycleView::next_attempt_at` rustdoc to replace the prose `backoff_duration` with the named constant `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` (e.g. \"computed from `tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`\" — match the rustdoc style of the existing field comments). (4) Run `cargo check -p overdrive-core --tests` — should now compile (Step 01-01's `unresolved import` is closed). Run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test acceptance` — Step 01-01's three tests now pass; all pre-existing tests in the same file also pass. (5) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace` — full default lane green. (6) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` on macOS to confirm the integration target still typechecks. (7) Inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI): run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`. Surface any test that now hits the new gate before reaching the count ceiling — per RCA §Risk, the named candidate is `repeatedly_crashing_workload_exhausts_backoff_and_stops_retrying`. If it (or any other) fails, adjust the test fixture's `tick.now` advancement to step past `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` between attempts; DO NOT weaken the assertion. Re-run until green. (8) Run `cargo xtask dst` — green. (9) Run `cargo xtask dst-lint` — green (the deadline write uses `tick.now` only, no `Instant::now()`). (10) Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` on `overdrive-core` — green. COMMIT — `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); the commit body explicitly lists the three production edits (constant, write, rustdoc) and confirms Step 01-01's three tests transition RED → GREEN within this commit. Single cohesive commit. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per testing.md §Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`. Invoke `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs`; wait for completion via the shell ID; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed: (a) `Duration::from_secs(1)` → `Duration::from_secs(0)` killed by Step 01-01 test (a)'s `next_view.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` assertion (with non-zero `RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION`, the assertion would fail); (b) deletion of the `next_attempt_at.insert(...)` line killed by Step 01-01 test (b)'s 'no second restart within window' assertion; (c) `tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` → `tick.now` (i.e. zero-duration deadline) killed by test (b) (the deadline elapses immediately, so tick-2 within the supposed window would see a stale deadline and re-emit); (d) `tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` → `previous_deadline + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` (rolled from prior deadline rather than current tick) killed by test (c)'s explicit `next_view_2.next_attempt_at[alloc_id] == new_tick.now + RESTART_BACKOFF_DURATION` assertion. On any surviving mutation, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it AND re-run mutation. Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome.
